rationale_for_baseline_tier: Painful perianal skin breakdown plus a faecal impaction with overflow in a bedbound, cognitively impaired frail elder requires a hands-on rectal examination, manual disimpaction and skin/continence care that telehealth cannot deliver — so an urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) is the correct level. The patient is currently afebrile with no obstruction, sepsis, new confusion or dehydrated obtundation, so this is not a T4-T5 emergency; the acute after-hours 'crisis/caregiver-burnout' framing must not inflate the tier. A conservative safety-net covers the emergency edges (sepsis, obstruction, dehydration/AKI) that a frail elder can decompensate into.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever at or above 38 degrees, shaking chills/rigors, or new or worsening confusion or drowsiness
escalation_edges.rationale: In a frail elder these signal possible sepsis (including from secondary skin infection) — a time-critical emergency; frail elders under-report so the threshold is deliberately low.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she spikes a fever, starts shivering, or becomes more confused or drowsy than usual, call 000 or go straight to the emergency department — this can be a serious infection.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Absolute stop in passing wind and stool, a worsening or hard swollen belly, severe generalised abdominal pain, or vomiting
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests progression to bowel obstruction from unrelieved impaction — needs emergency assessment and imaging.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she completely stops passing wind and stool, her belly becomes hard and swollen or very painful, or she starts vomiting, take her to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Perianal redness spreading rapidly beyond the buttocks, skin breaking down, or the area becoming hot and hardened
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals possible cellulitis / progressive tissue breakdown requiring same-day in-person review and possible antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads quickly, the skin breaks down further, or the area turns hot and firm, she needs to be seen in person the same day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Passing little or no urine, becoming very drowsy or hard to rouse, or not able to be woken normally
escalation_edges.rationale: Reduced intake plus fluid loss risks dehydration and acute kidney injury; obtundation or anuria is an emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she is barely passing urine, or becomes very drowsy and hard to wake, call 000 — she may be badly dehydrated.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New rectal bleeding, unexplained weight loss, or a persistent change in bowel habit after the impaction is cleared
escalation_edges.rationale: Altered bowel habit in an older adult mandates exclusion of colorectal malignancy on the fast-track pathway.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If there's any bleeding from the back passage, unexplained weight loss, or her bowels stay changed once the blockage is cleared, book a prompt GP review for further bowel investigations.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Primary caregiver at the point of being unable to cope with continuous soiling and pad changes
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise a home-deliverable in-person route (after-hours/community nursing) and arrange skin-care support and respite; the plan must be achievable for an overwhelmed sole caregiver.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Frail, cognitively impaired, bedbound older adult who under-reports and presents atypically
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Keep a low threshold to escalate on any new confusion, fever, reduced intake or reduced urine output — an acute change in a frail elder is an emergency until a reversible/serious cause is excluded.
Route the in-person assessment via a home visit / community nursing rather than clinic attendance; if an emergency edge triggers, arrange ambulance transport.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This needs an in-person assessment today: someone needs to examine her back passage, clear the blockage and treat the raw skin — this cannot be done safely over a chat, and anti-diarrhoea medicines must not be used because the leaking is coming from a blockage. Arrange an urgent same-day home visit or GP/community-nursing review, keep the skin clean and dry with barrier cream and breathable pads, and offer small sips of water.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person review via after-hours GP home visit or community/continence nursing; call the after-hours line to arrange.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If any emergency warning sign appears (fever/rigors, new confusion, severe belly pain or no wind/stool, spreading skin redness, or little/no urine), call 000 or go to the emergency department without waiting for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document the overflow-impaction pattern, the opioid-without-aperient mechanism, the perianal skin breakdown, vitals reported, the disimpaction and skin protocol, the bowel regimen and medication-review recommendation, and the safety-net thresholds.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as routine self-care or a routine GP booking, or managing it as simple diarrhoea, and missing the impaction, skin breakdown and the frail-elder emergency windows.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Unrelieved impaction progresses to obstruction/stercoral complications; untreated skin breakdown becomes cellulitis/sepsis; dehydration/AKI develops unrecognised.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Booking only a prompt (24-48h) GP review without arranging the same-day hands-on examination and disimpaction the painful skin breakdown warrants.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending a stable, afebrile patient with no emergency red flag straight to 000/ED, driven by the acute 'crisis' framing or caregiver distress rather than the clinical picture.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The impaction cannot be confirmed, and obstruction/malignancy cannot be excluded, without an in-person rectal examination (and, if indicated, bloods/imaging) that telehealth cannot provide.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Name the strong presumptive diagnosis, explain it is not a stomach bug, arrange the urgent in-person examination/disimpaction, and give the conservative red-flag safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure and defer, manage as infective diarrhoea, recommend an antidiarrhoeal, or instruct home disimpaction.
